data.title: Retail rundown: Destination XL combines with FullBeauty in a merger of equals
data.description:
Destination XL Group (DXLG) is merging with privately held FullBeauty Brands in a merger of equals combination that will create one of the largest North American players focused on inclusive and extended-size apparel. 

Following a comprehensive review of the transaction, the Destination XL Group (DXLG) board determined that the merger has the potential to create significant value for the company and is the best path forward for shareholders. 

FullBeauty shareholders are expected to own roughly 55% of the combined company and existing DXLG shareholders about 45% at closing. The transaction is structured so that FullBeauty will be merged into a newly formed subsidiary of Destination XL (DXLG), and the combined entity will remain a publicly traded company under the DXLG ticker

The combined company will unite complementary brands, channels, and capabilities to better serve plus-size women and Big + Tall men as a larger, stronger, and more flexible public company positioned for long-term growth. Together, Destination XL (DXLG) and FullBeauty will be a leader in inclusive sizing apparel with one of the industry’s broadest and most diverse portfolios, spanning value to premium across lifestyles and occasions. By leveraging complementary strengths across gender, product and channel, the combined company is anticipated to be positioned to accelerate growth, improve operational efficiency, and deliver an enhanced customer experience through a comprehensive, innovative multi-channel strategy. The combined company will reflect a direct-to-consumer mix at 73% of total sales, with bricks-and-mortar at 27% of total sales.

The companies delivered combined sales of approximately $1.2 billion for the last twelve months ending October 2025. Assuming no pro forma adjustments, adjusted EBITDA was approximately $45 million. Including $25 million in expected annual run-rate cost synergies, the combined company would have generated approximately $70 million of LTM adjusted EBITDA.

Destination XL (DXLG) has been a public company for decades. Designs, Inc. (the original corporate name) went public in 1987 and ultimately evolved into today’s Destination XL Group (DXLG). After the 2002 Casual Male acquisition, the listing continued under Casual Male Retail Group, and in December 2012, the Nasdaq ticker was changed to DXLG, followed in February 2013 by a formal corporate name change from Casual Male Retail Group  to Destination XL Group to reflect the strategic pivot to the DXL concept.

Destination XL (DXLG) competes with mass retailers like Target (TGT), Walmart (WM), and Old Navy (GAP) by offering big-and-tall or extended sizes within broader men’s assortments, often at lower price points. Following the merger, the combined company will also compete against Lane Bryant and Torrid.

data.title: S&P, Nasdaq 100 futures drop as Broadcom guidance puts AI trade under pressure
data.description:
Stock index futures pointed to a mixed open Friday, as AI momentum hit another speed bump.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged down -0.14% to 6,891.10, and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) fell more sharply by -0.59% to 25,536.26. Dow futures (INDU) bucked the trend, gaining +0.21% to 48,804.72.

The AI trade came under pressure after chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) warned of margin pressures and disappointed investors with a lesser-than-expected backlog figure for AI product orders. 

"The swing in AI sentiment continued with Broadcom’s (AVGO) earnings after the US close," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. "Following a +78% advance YTD, the chipmaker now has a larger market cap than Meta and Tesla, playing a growing role in the tech market narrative." 

"The stock initially advanced +4% after-hours after unveiling stronger-than-expected revenue guidance for the current quarter ($19.1bn vs $18.5bn est.). But it then turned sharply lower as management held off on giving an AI revenue forecast for the year," Reid added.

In the Treasury market, yields showed varied movements across different maturities. The U.S. 2-Year Treasury yield (US2Y) decreased by -0.17% to 3.55%, while the 10-Year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved in the opposite direction, rising +0.22% to 4.17%. The longer-dated 30-Year yield (US30Y) was up +0.44% to 4.82%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included lululemon athletica (LULU) with a substantial rise of +9.88%, Newmont (NEM) climbing +2.78%, and Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) advancing +2.62%. On the downside, notable decliners were led by Broadcom (AVGO) dropping -5.76%, followed by Sandisk (SNDK) falling -3.59%, and Fastenal (FAST) declining -2.95%.

data.title: Citigroup upgraded to Overweight at J.P. Morgan on expected boost from economy, markets
data.description:
J. P. Morgan upgraded Citigroup (C) to Overweight from Neutral on Friday as analyst Vivek Juneja expects the bank to benefit relatively more from a solid economy and strong markets-related activity due to its concentration of revenues.

In addition, Citi (C) should continue to reap benefits from its transformation through an improving efficiency ratio, progress on consent orders, reducing stranded costs, and reduction of DTA.

"These should continue to improve Citi's (C) profitability over time and increase RoTCE more than peers," Juneja added. 

J.P. Morgan boosts its price target to $124 from $107. The valuation reflects a price/tangible book value of ~1.1x JPM's estimated 2027 TBV and a price/earnings multiple of 11.0x estimated 2027 EPS, a discount to peers due to the bank's lower profitability.

Citigroup (C) stock rose 1.3% in premarket trading.

The Overweight rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and agrees with the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Gen Z protests bring down a European government for the first time
data.description:
Gen Z protests have brought down Bulgaria's government, the first such instance in Europe after similar youth-led uprisings toppled regimes around the world this year.

Bulgaria's government resigned on Thursday, just weeks before the Balkan nation was set to join the eurozone - a group of EU countries that adopted the euro as their currency.

Tens of thousands of Bulgarians took the streets this month to protest ongoing corruption and the government's 2026 budget plan.

The proposal included higher taxes and increased social security contributions to fund more public spending. The government withdrew its plan following widespread pushback.

Bulgarian Prime Minister Rosen Zhelyazkov announced his government's resignation minutes before the parliament was due to vote on a no-confidence motion from the opposition. Notably, Zhelyazkov's government survived six no-confidence votes since it was appointed in January.

Bulgaria is ranked as the EU's second most corrupt member state, after Hungary, according to Transparency International's Corruption Perceptions Index 2024. 

"The protests may have started because of the budget, but actually it's about where there is corruption in the government," Dimitar Keranov, a Bulgarian analyst at the German Marshall Fund, told The New York Times. He said many Bulgarians believe corruption is being perpetuated by politicians who favor alignment with Russia.

-- The Associated Press contributed to this report.

data.title: Yum China puts $1.5B in buybacks and dividends on the menu for 2026
data.description:
Yum China Holdings (YUMC) announced on Friday that the restaurant company entered into share repurchase agreements in the U.S. and Hong Kong for an aggregate repurchase amount of approximately $460 million for the first half of 2026. The buybacks are slated to begin on January 12, 2026.

The share repurchase agreements cover approximately $350 million in the U.S. and approximately HK$880 million in Hong Kong. The agreements are one component of the share repurchase program and part of the broader plan to return $1.5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases in 2026. The company highlighted that the annual amount of capital return is equivalent to around 9% of its current market capitalization

"Yum China is committed to maintaining a dual focus: investing to drive business growth and delivering favorable capital returns to shareholders. Supported by a healthy cash position and strong cash-generation capabilities, we are on track to return US$4.5 billion to shareholders from 2024 through 2026. Starting in 2027, as outlined at our 2025 Investor Day, we plan to return approximately 100% of annual free cash flow after subsidiaries' dividend payments to non-controlling interests," stated Yum China (YUMC) CEO Joey Wat.

Shares of Yum China (YUMC) were up 1.0% in premarket trading.

data.title: FDA plans to put “black box” warning on Covid vaccines: report
data.description:
The US Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) plans to add a “black box” warning to COVID-19 vaccines, CNN reported, citing two people familiar with the decision.

A boxed warning—the FDA’s most serious alert—highlights risks such as death or life-threatening or disabling reactions that must be weighed against a medication’s benefits.

The move to add boxed warnings to COVID-19 vaccines is being led by Dr. Vinay Prasad, the FDA's chief medical and scientific officer and director of the agency's Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research, the report added.

The plan is not yet final and could still change. It’s unclear whether the proposed warnings—expected by year-end—would apply only to mRNA vaccines or to all COVID-19 vaccines, and whether they would affect every age group. Of the three FDA-approved Covid vaccines in the U.S., two—Pfizer (PFE) and Moderna (MRNA) —use mRNA technology, which has been a central focus for the administration.

Moderna (MRNA) co-founder and chairman Noubar Afeyan recently defended mRNA vaccines amid an FDA probe into potential vaccine-related deaths, emphasizing that extensive studies show strong safety data and that the shots are estimated to have prevented nearly 20 million deaths globally in their first year — more than a million in the U.S. He urged regulators to focus on “facts and evidence.”

The FDA is "doing a thorough investigation, across multiple age groups, of deaths potentially related to Covid vaccines," an agency spokesperson told Bloomberg earlier this month.

Bloomberg also reported that the FDA is examining safety data for RSV shots in infants. Sanofi's (SNY) Beyfortus (nirsevimab) and Merck's (MRK) Enflonsia (clesrovimab) are approved for this population.

data.title: Gaming companies make a last-ditch effort to reverse law taxing 'phantom' winnings
data.description:
The 90% limit on deductibility of gambling losses is scheduled to take effect with the first tax year beginning January 1, 2026. Despite the proximity to the law being in effect, there is a late‑stage push from lawmakers, gaming industry groups, and casino operators to restore full 100% offsetting of losses against winnings. If the effort to repeal the "phantom income" tax fails, it could be especially harmful to high‑volume and professional bettors.​

The development means that if a gambler wins $100,000 in a tax year while also losing $100,000, they would be required to pay taxes on $10,000, despite breaking even on a cash basis. The concern is that if the bill passes with the gambling provision in place, many high-stakes gamblers will opt to place their action with offshore, illegal operators. Analysts and industry insiders have also warned that the prospect of paying taxes on "phantom" winnings may discourage both professional and recreational gamblers from participating, potentially leading to a decline in betting activity and overall casino and sportsbook revenue.

Under current U.S. tax law, individuals are required to report all gambling winnings as taxable income. Casinos and other gambling establishments issue W-2G forms for certain gambling winnings. That includes $1,200 or more from slots or bingo, $1,500 or more from keno, $5,000 or more from poker tournaments, and $600 for sports betting for a payout at least 300X the original bet. Even if a gambler does not receive a W-2G because they are below the thresholds, they are required to report gambling winnings on their tax form.

On the House side, Democrat Representative Dina Titus of Nevada has been the most visible champion of repeal, introducing the FAIR BET Act (H.R. 4304) to restore the pre‑OBBBA rule and allow gamblers to deduct 100% of losses against winnings. Titus, joined by co‑sponsors from both parties, has argued that the 90% cap forces taxpayers to pay federal income tax on money they never actually keep, and has worked to keep the bill in play during year‑end tax negotiations. Just this week, the CEOs of MGM Resorts (MGM), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Caesars Entertainment (CZR), and Circa Sports met with the American Gaming Association to create grassroots support for preventing the 90% cap on gaming loss deductions from becoming a reality.
